name: Drawer
visual_summary: A reusable Drawer UI component that can be used as a sidebar or overlay to draw user attention. It is suitable for displaying navigation, tools, or other contextual information. When a developer would use it? When they want to provide additional context or allow user interaction without taking full screen.
generation_prompt:
Create a production-ready card component with the following characteristics:

Name: Drawer
Description: A reusable Drawer UI component that can be used as a sidebar or overlay to draw user attention. It is suitable for displaying navigation, tools, or other contextual information. When a developer would use it? When they want to provide additional context or allow user interaction without taking full screen.
Style: minimal
Interactions: expandable, collapsible
Tags: drawer, sidebar, overlay, navigation, context

Requirements:
- Works in React with TypeScript by default but adaptable to Vue, Svelte, or plain HTML/CSS
- Uses Tailwind CSS for styling
- Fully accessible with ARIA labels and keyboard navigation
- Responsive and mobile-friendly
- No external dependencies beyond React and Tailwind unless necessary
- Include all necessary imports
- Export as default with sensible prop types

Generate clean production-ready code only. No explanations.
Reference implementation (adapt don't copy):
// src/components/Drawer/Drawer.tsx
// Tremor Drawer [v1.0.0]

import * as React from "react"
import * as DrawerPrimitives from "@radix-ui/react-dialog"
import { RiCloseLine } from "@remixicon/react"

import { cx } from "../../utils/cx"
import { focusRing } from "../../utils/focusRing"
import { Button } from "../Button/Button"

const Drawer = (
  props: React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof DrawerPrimitives.Root>,
) => {
  return <DrawerPrimitives.Root tremor-id="tremor-raw" {...props} />
}
Draw
